 {"id":"bd-e166","title":"Improve timestamp comparison readability in import","description":"The timestamp comparison logic uses double-negative which can be confusing:

Current code:
if !incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // skip update
}

More readable:
if incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // perform update
} else {
    // skip (local is newer)
}

This is a minor refactor for code clarity.

Related: bd-1022
Files: internal/importer/importer.go:411, 488","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-02T15:32:12.27108-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T18:38:37.688877572-05:00","closed_at":"2025-11-26T22:25:27.124071-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-e16b","title":"Replace BEADS_DB with BEADS_DIR environment variable","description":"Implement BEADS_DIR as a replacement for BEADS_DB to point to the .beads directory instead of the database file directly.

Rationale:
- With --no-db mode, there's no .db file to point to
- The .beads directory is the logical unit (contains config.yaml, db files, jsonl files)
- More intuitive: point to the beads directory not the database file

Implementation:
1. Add BEADS_DIR environment variable support
2. Maintain backward compatibility with BEADS_DB
3. Priority order: BEADS_DIR > BEADS_DB > auto-discovery
4. If BEADS_DIR is set, look for config.yaml in that directory to find actual database path
5. Update documentation and migration guide

Files to modify:
- beads.go (FindDatabasePath function)
- cmd/bd/main.go (initialization)
- Documentation (CLI_REFERENCE.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md, etc.)
- MCP integration (integrations/beads-mcp/src/beads_mcp/config.py)

Testing:
- Ensure BEADS_DB still works (backward compatibility)
- Test BEADS_DIR with both db and --no-db modes
- Test priority order when both are set
- Update integration tests

-{"id":"bd-e1bp5","title":"Review & merge PR #1013: Hyphenated prefix support","description":"## PR Review Task

**PR:** https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/pull/1013
**Author:** sukhodolin

## Summary
Fix ValidateIDFormat to support hyphenated prefixes (e.g., 'bead-me-up-3e9').

## Changes
- internal/validation/bead.go: Use utils.ExtractIssuePrefix instead of naive first-hyphen split
- internal/validation/bead_test.go: Add comprehensive test cases

## The Bug
Before: 'bead-me-up-3e9' → prefix 'bead' (wrong)
After: 'bead-me-up-3e9' → prefix 'bead-me-up' (correct)

This fixes `bd create --parent` failing for projects with hyphenated prefixes.

## Review Checklist
1. Pull branch locally: `gh pr checkout 1013 --repo steveyegge/beads`
2. Run tests: `go test ./internal/validation/...`
3. Verify all new test cases pass
4. If all good, merge: `gh pr merge 1013 --repo steveyegge/beads --squash`

## Classification
Easy-win: Bug fix using existing utility, excellent test coverage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-11T18:17:33.24321-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-11T18:17:33.24321-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-e2e6","title":"Implement postinstall script for binary download","description":"Create npm/scripts/postinstall.js that downloads platform-specific binaries:

## Platform detection
- Detect os.platform() and os.arch()
- Map to GitHub release asset names:
  - linux-amd64 → bd-linux-amd64
  - linux-arm64 → bd-linux-arm64
  - darwin-amd64 → bd-darwin-amd64
  - darwin-arm64 → bd-darwin-arm64
  - win32-x64 → bd-windows-amd64.exe

## Download logic
- Fetch from GitHub releases: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/releases/latest/download/${asset}
- Save to npm/bin/bd (or bd.exe on Windows)
- Set executable permissions (chmod +x)
- Handle errors gracefully with helpful messages

## Error handling
- Check for unsupported platforms
- Retry on network failures
- Provide manual download instructions if automated fails
- Skip download if binary already exists (for local development)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:39:56.652829-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-03T10:31:45.382215-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-03T10:31:45.382215-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-e2e6","depends_on_id":"bd-febc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:40:32.94671-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-e2q9","title":"bd sync fails when run from directory with beads redirect","description":"
## Bug

`bd sync` fails with 'git status failed in worktree: exit status 128' when run from a directory that uses a beads redirect (e.g., crew/gus/.beads/redirect pointing to mayor/rig/.beads).

## Reproduction

1. Be in gastown/crew/gus (has .beads/redirect → ../../mayor/rig/.beads)
2. Run `bd sync`
3. Error: 'git status failed in worktree: exit status 128'

## Workaround

Run `bd sync` from the actual beads location:
```bash
cd /Users/stevey/gt/gastown/mayor/rig && bd sync
```

## Root Cause

The worktree path resolution in `getBeadsWorktreePath()` likely uses the crew/gus repo root instead of following the redirect to mayor/rig. The git worktree for beads-sync is in mayor/rig/.git/beads-worktrees/, not crew/gus/.git/beads-worktrees/.

## Impact

Crew workers with redirected beads cannot sync directly from their workspace.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-31T11:58:39.875764-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/gus","updated_at":"2025-12-31T13:31:39.510735-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T13:31:39.510735-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: use getRepoRootFromPath(ctx, beadsDir) instead of syncbranch.GetRepoRoot(ctx) to resolve repo root from redirect target"}

 {"id":"bd-febc","title":"npm package for bd with native binaries","description":"Create an npm package that wraps native bd binaries for easy installation in Claude Code for Web and other Node.js environments.

## Problem
Claude Code for Web sandboxes are full Linux VMs with npm support, but cannot easily download binaries from GitHub releases due to network restrictions or tooling limitations.

## Solution
Publish bd as an npm package that:
- Downloads platform-specific native binaries during postinstall
- Provides a CLI wrapper that invokes the native binary
- Works seamlessly in Claude Code for Web SessionStart hooks
- Maintains full feature parity (uses native SQLite)

## Benefits vs WASM
- ✅ Full SQLite support (no custom VFS needed)
- ✅ All features work identically to native bd
- ✅ Better performance (native vs WASM overhead)
- ✅ ~4 hours effort vs ~2 days for WASM
- ✅ Minimal maintenance burden

## Success Criteria
- npm install @beads/bd works in Claude Code for Web
- All bd commands function identically to native binary
- SessionStart hook documented for auto-installation
- Package published to npm registry","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:39:37.684109-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-03T10:39:44.932565-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-03T10:39:44.932565-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-fedb","title":"Polecats should spawn with auto-accept mode enabled","description":"During swarm bd-784c, polecats (Toast, Nux) were spawned without --dangerously-skip-permissions or equivalent auto-accept mode.

**Problem:**
Every edit, bash command, and tool use required manual confirmation via tmux send-keys. This defeats the purpose of autonomous polecat operation.

**Expected:**
Polecats in a swarm should run with permissions bypassed so they can work autonomously.

**Workaround used:**
- Manually sent Enter keys via tmux to accept prompts
- Eventually polecats entered 'bypass permissions on' mode after restart

**Suggestion:**
- gt sling should pass --dangerously-skip-permissions by default for polecats
- Or polecats should have a .claude/settings.local.json pre-configured for auto-accept","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-28T16:17:47.061327-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-28T22:14:19.109728-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T22:14:19.109728-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-fej5","title":"bd hook: detect agent from cwd instead of defaulting to $USER","description":"**Problem:**
`bd hook` without `--agent` defaults to `$USER` (e.g., "stevey") instead of detecting the agent identity from the current working directory.

**Expected behavior:**
When running from `/Users/stevey/gt/beads/crew/dave`, the agent should be detected as `beads/crew/dave`.

**Current behavior:**
```bash
$ pwd
/Users/stevey/gt/beads/crew/dave
$ bd hook
Hook: stevey
  (empty)

$ bd hook --agent beads/crew/dave
Hook: beads/crew/dave
  📌 bd-hobo (mol) - open
```

**Fix:**
bd hook should use the same cwd-based agent detection that other commands use (similar to how `gt mail` determines identity from cwd).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T20:01:27.613892-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T12:41:10.65257-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T12:41:10.65257-08:00"}
